Fit a robust model for a continuous variable

Source: R/robust_conditional.R
fit_robust_continuous.Rd

Fits a robust regression model for sequential conditional synthesis. MM-estimation (robustbase::lmrob) is the default. When method = "robust_cart", a robust CART model is fitted via partykit::ctree (if available) or rpart::rpart with Huber-weighted splits as fallback.

Usage

fit_robust_continuous(
  y,
  X,
  method = c("mm", "robust_cart"),
  weights = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

y

numeric response vector

X

data.frame of predictors (already synthesised variables)

method

"mm" (default) or "robust_cart"

weights

optional cell-level weights: a list with components w_response (length n) and w_predictors (n x p_pred matrix) coming from estimate_contamination. Leave as NULL to use lmrob's built-in M-weights only.

...

additional arguments passed to lmrob or rpart/ctree

Value

A list of class "robsynth_fit_cont" with components:

model

the fitted model object

method

the fitting method used

sigma

robust residual scale estimate

y_obs

observed response (for PMM)

yhat_obs

fitted values (for PMM)

Author

Matthias Templ

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
fit <- fit_robust_continuous(iris$Sepal.Width,
                             iris[, "Sepal.Length", drop = FALSE])
} # }

On this page
